step_2_1_clipboard_flavors.method: authored Heading-1 + bold/red content in a scratch doc, copy object (text object of d), read `clipboard info`
step_2_1_clipboard_flavors.verdict: Word publishes HTML + RTF + plain text (+ PDF/webarchive) on every rich copy. Our serializer must publish at least text/html + text/plain; the clone's Paste Special offers HTML / Unformatted Text / Picture classes accordingly.
step_2_1_clipboard_flavors.html_fidelity_note: h1 preserves Heading 1; <b> preserves bold; color:red span preserves font color — leg-B paste of Word HTML into the clone should map these via the PM clipboardParser (Heading1 style + bold mark + color mark).
step_2_1_clipboard_flavors.plain_text_payload:
Heading sample line
plain boldword redword tail

step_2_2_paste_menus.method: Codex computer-use: Paste Special dialog per clipboard state (screenshots A2/A4/A6 corroborated); ribbon Paste-arrow menu NOT capturable (transient layer).
step_2_2_paste_menus.pasteLinkRadio: present but DISABLED in all three states
step_2_2_paste_menus.verdict: D.pasteSpecial flavor-from-clipboard CONFIRMED. Clone classes map: HTML Format→pasteHTML, Unformatted Text→pasteTextOnly, Picture→pastePicture. Mac ribbon Paste-ARROW item composition UNCONFIRMED — keep the plan's flavor-gated menu, recorded deviation, re-check in Task 10/user-visual.
step_2_2_paste_menus.editMenu_states: rich+text+image all show Paste enabled, Paste Special enabled; Paste-and-Match-Formatting disabled for plain text.

step_2_3_painter_matrix.method: Codex computer-use; Format Painter BUTTON refused by Computer-Use, so Word native `copy/paste format selection` (same engine) with AppleScript property readbacks.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B1_charFromWord: target got bold+red ONLY; style/align/spacing untouched — char-scope capture from a word.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B2_wholeParagraph: target became Heading 1 + centered + space-before 12 + Heading-1 char appearance — paragraph formatting AND style transfer. CONFIRMS captureParaProps carries styleId/justification/spacing.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B3_caretOnly: CONFIRMED caret-arming: target got Heading 1 + centered + spacing from a mere caret. Keep armPainterPM caret default (no empty-selection refusal).
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B4_sticky: two-paint state both bold; the double-click/Esc gesture itself NOT observed — sticky/Esc assumed standard.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B5_chords: native copy/paste-format equivalent → dst bold. Supports ⌘⇧C/⌘⇧V = painter (Task 8 chords).
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B6_replaceVsMerge: CONFIRMED REPLACE: dst was italic+underline, became bold ONLY (italic+underline removed). Validates clear-then-apply.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B7_listMembership: NOT OBSERVED (bullet source couldn't be created via Computer-Use/AppleScript). Keep plan default: numbering CARRIED; validate in Task 10.
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B8_hyperlink: CONFIRMED link survives paint-OVER (hyperlink count stayed 1). Validates excluding `link` mark from the clear. Copy-FROM-link-to-plain unconfirmed (we also exclude link from CAPTURE, so safe).
step_2_3_painter_matrix.B9_mixedSource: FINDING: selecting BOTH a bold + a plain word then painting → dst became BOLD (first-run formatting), NOT intersection-empty. REFINE Task 3: for a non-empty selection capture marks at selection.from (first char), not getMarksFromSelection (which intersects mixed→[]). Anticipated by plan probe-9 note.

task9_manual_sanity.method: launched the built clone (npx electron .), drove real OS keystrokes via System Events, screencaptured the window
task9_manual_sanity.physical_cmd_v_into_clone: PASS — 'PHYSCLIP_PASTE_OK' (from pbcopy, another app) pasted into the PM editor via Blink-native Cmd+V; no hidden menu needed (confirms the Task-8 deferred verdict).
task9_manual_sanity.physical_cmd_c_out_of_clone: PASS — Select-All + Cmd+C overwrote a poisoned clipboard with the doc content (cross-app copy-out works).
task9_manual_sanity.five_gates: PM 112/112, legacy 257/257, smoke 9/9 x2, docx 17/17 (clean build).
task9_manual_sanity.covered_by_pm_suite: ribbon cut/copy/paste, paste flavors (KSF/KeepTextOnly/Picture), Paste Special dialog, format painter one-shot/sticky/Esc/button-chrome, Select All, Similar Formatting — all green.
task9_manual_sanity.wired_not_visually_reconfirmed: Cmd+Shift+C/V copy/paste-format chords (oracle B5, bridge-wired); painter copy-cursor (cosmetic). Low risk.

legA_clone_to_word.method: clone Select-All+Cmd+C → inspected clipboard HTML → physical Cmd+V into real Word → screenshot
legA_clone_to_word.clone_html_word_compatible:
YES — , MSO @list styles,

with markers; data-superdoc-slice blob is display:none (internal round-trip).
legA_clone_to_word.word_render: real Word (Document5) rendered the full list structure: top-level bullets (-), nested sub-bullet (◦ Indentation 1), numbered list with nested alpha (a. With indent 1) — markers + nesting + bullet-vs-numbered all correct. Screenshot legA-word-paste.png.
legA_clone_to_word.applescript_style_readback: blocked by session degradation (quirk #26 object flicker) — visual screenshot used instead.

legB_word_to_clone.method: Word Select-All+Cmd+C → clone bridge readHTML()+pasteHTML() probe → inspect PM doc
legB_word_to_clone.core: PASS — 9 paragraphs, ListParagraph styleId, numbering ilvl 0/1 (nesting) all survive Word's 46KB MSO-list HTML through the PM parser.
legB_word_to_clone.DEVIATION: Word's supportLists literal markers (-, ◦, 1., a.) leak in as TEXT alongside the engine's own numbering (numberingProperties.ilvl set) → DOUBLE MARKER on paste of Word lists. Word strips its own `mso-list:Ignore` / <!--[if !supportLists]--> marker spans on import; the clone's PM HTML parser does not. The clone EMITS these markers (for Word compat, leg A) but does not STRIP them on import.
legB_word_to_clone.fix_scope: fork super-converter HTML import (strip mso-list:Ignore / supportLists marker spans) — delicate (shared with file-open), out of slice-4 paste scope; recorded follow-up (candidate: slice 7 converter work). Simple/plain HTML paste (the [4] KSF test) is unaffected.
legB_word_to_clone.CAVEAT: The leg-B content ORIGINATED from the clone (clone→Word→clone round-trip), so Word's clipboard HTML still carried the clone's data-superdoc-slice blob. Whether NATIVE Word-authored list HTML (no slice blob, pure mso-list markup) leaks markers the same way is UNCONFIRMED — couldn't author fresh Word list content this session (Word session degraded to 0 docs / quirk #24 + osascript assistive-access flicker). Follow-up: confirm with native Word content, then fix (candidate: strip <!--[if !supportLists]-->/mso-list:Ignore marker spans in bridge pasteHTML preprocessing — OUR code, view.pasteHTML uses the PM clipboard parser not the OOXML converter — or in the converter import).
